title: Buttery Juicy Polish Pork Pancakes
description: A traditional Polish dish featuring rice flour pancakes filled with juicy pork and topped with melted butter, perfect for a hangover cure. This paleo-friendly recipe uses blanched pork for added tenderness.
instructions: In a large bowl, whisk together rice flour, eggs, and a pinch of salt.
Heat a non-stick skillet over medium heat and add 1 tablespoon of melted butter.
Using a 1/4 cup measuring cup, scoop the batter into the skillet and cook for 2-3 minutes or until bubbles appear on the surface.
Flip the pancake and cook for an additional 1-2 minutes or until golden brown.
In a separate pan, heat 1 tablespoon of melted butter over medium heat and add the diced pork, cooking until browned and crispy.
Add the chopped onion and minced garlic to the pan and cook until the onion is translucent.
To assemble the dish, place a cooked pancake on a plate, add a spoonful of the pork mixture, and top with melted butter.
Repeat the process until all ingredients are used up.
Cook pork to an internal temperature of 63°C / 145°F and let it rest for 3 minutes before serving.
